title: Chinese Firm Tracks B-2 Bombers Returning from Iran Mission via OSINT
summary: A Chinese technology company reconstructed flight paths of four B-2 stealth bombers returning from an Iran mission using OSINT and AI tools. Analysts integrated open-source intelligence with situational awareness systems to map routes from call signs Petro 41 to Petro 44. The claim highlights advancements in tracking stealth aircraft during operations like Epic Fury.
contents:
Jingqi system operators at the Chinese firm pieced together online traces to reconstruct B-2 return paths from Iran on March 10, 2026. Four bombers, identified by call signs Petro 41 through Petro 44, departed after Day 2 tasks in Operation Epic Fury. AI-driven analysis fused satellite imagery, flight tracking data, and social media geolocations to verify trajectories.

Experts demonstrated precision in plotting routes back to U.S. bases, challenging stealth capabilities. The firm integrated OSINT from global networks, including amateur radio intercepts and ADS-B signals. Videos surfaced online showing real-time path reconstructions, sparking debates on modern warfare transparency.

Military analysts reported implications for conflict zone verification, as similar tools enable troop movement tracking without classified intel. Chinese developers emphasized non-intrusive methods, relying solely on public data streams. Skeptics noted B-2s maintain operational security despite such efforts.

Geolocation techniques pinpointed waypoints over the Middle East, aligning with reported strike timelines. The demonstration underscores AI's role in OSINT for intelligence gathering. Observers monitored similar applications in ongoing regional tensions.

Broader adoption of these systems promises enhanced verification in disputes. Firms continue refining algorithms for real-time analysis.
